document.description: Elements that define the document structure and metadata.

html.description: The root element of an HTML document. All other elements must be descendants of this element. The `lang` attribute declares the language of the page.

head.description: Contains machine-readable information (metadata) about the document, such as title, scripts, and style sheets.

body.description: Represents the content of an HTML document. There can be only one `<body>` element per document.

title.description: Defines the document's title shown in the browser's title bar or tab. Required in all HTML documents. Important for SEO and accessibility.

meta.description: Represents metadata that cannot be expressed by other meta-related elements (base, link, script, style, title). Used for charset, viewport, description, keywords, and Open Graph.

link.description: Specifies relationships between the current document and external resources. Most commonly used for stylesheets, favicons, and resource hints (preconnect, preload, prefetch).

script.description: Embeds executable code or references an external script file. Use `defer` or `async` to control loading behavior. `type="module"` enables ES modules.

style.description: Contains CSS styling rules for the document. Placed in `<head>`. For large stylesheets, prefer external `<link>` for cacheability.

base.description: Specifies the base URL and/or default target for all relative URLs in the document. There can be only one `<base>` per document.

noscript.description: Defines content to be displayed if the browser does not support scripting or has scripts disabled. Can appear in both `<head>` and `<body>`.

sectioning.description: Elements that define the sections and structure of an HTML document.

header.description: Represents introductory content or a group of navigational aids. Can contain headings, logos, search forms, author names. Not limited to top of page — usable in `<article>` or `<section>`.

footer.description: Represents a footer for its nearest sectioning content or sectioning root element. Typically contains author info, copyright data, or related links.

nav.description: Represents a section with navigation links. Intended for major navigation blocks (site nav, table of contents). Not all link groups need `<nav>`.

main.description: Represents the dominant content of the `<body>`. Only one visible `<main>` per page. Content unique to the page — excludes repeated elements like sidebars and navs.

section.description: Represents a standalone section of content. Each section should typically have a heading. Use when content forms a logical group.

article.description: Represents a self-contained composition intended for independent distribution or reuse: blog posts, news articles, forum posts, product cards, user comments.

aside.description: Represents content tangentially related to surrounding content. Common uses: sidebars, pull quotes, advertising, groups of related links.

h1.description: Top-level heading. Use only one per page. Critical for SEO and document outline.

h2.description: Second-level heading. Used for major sections under the page title.

h3.description: Third-level heading. Used for sub-sections within an `<h2>` section.

hgroup.description:
Groups a heading with secondary content such as subheadings, alternative titles, or taglines. Contains one heading element (h1–h6) and optionally `

` elements.

address.description: Provides contact information for the nearest `<article>` or `<body>` ancestor. Typically rendered in italic. Not for arbitrary postal addresses.

text_content.description: Elements used for organizing blocks of text content within the body.

p.description: Represents a paragraph of text. Browsers add vertical margins by default. Cannot contain block-level elements.

div.description: Generic container for flow content. Has no semantic meaning. Use when no other semantic element (article, section, nav, etc.) is appropriate.

span.description: Generic inline container for phrasing content. Used for styling or grouping when no semantic element fits.

pre.description: Represents preformatted text. Whitespace is displayed exactly as written. Commonly used with `<code>` for code blocks.

blockquote.description: Indicates an extended quotation from another source. The `cite` attribute provides the source URL. Typically indented by browsers.

ol.description: Represents an ordered list. Items are numbered. Supports `start`, `type` (1, a, A, i, I), and `reversed` attributes.

ul.description: Represents an unordered list of items, typically rendered with bullet points.

li.description: Represents a list item. Must be a direct child of `<ul>`, `<ol>`, or `<menu>`. In `<ol>`, the `value` attribute overrides numbering.

dl.description: Represents a description list — a list of term-description groups. Contains `<dt>` (term) and `<dd>` (description) pairs.

dt.description: Specifies a term in a description list (`<dl>`). Usually followed by one or more `<dd>` elements.

dd.description: Provides the description or definition for the preceding `<dt>` in a description list.

figure.description: Represents self-contained content, optionally with a caption (`<figcaption>`). Used for images, illustrations, diagrams, code listings.

figcaption.description: Represents a caption or legend for a `<figure>` element. Should be the first or last child of `<figure>`.

hr.description: Represents a thematic break between paragraph-level elements. Rendered as a horizontal line by default.

inline_text.description: Elements that define the meaning, structure, or style of a word, line, or piece of text.

a.description: Creates a hyperlink. The `href` attribute specifies the link destination. Use `target="_blank"` with `rel="noopener"` for external links.

em.description: Marks text that has stress emphasis. Renders italic by default. Nesting `<em>` increases emphasis level.

strong.description: Indicates strong importance, seriousness, or urgency. Renders bold by default.

small.description: Represents side comments and small print, like copyright and legal text.

s.description: Represents text that is no longer accurate or relevant (strikethrough). Not for document edits — use `<del>` instead.

cite.description: Represents the title of a creative work (book, film, song). Renders italic by default.

q.description: Represents a short inline quotation. Browsers automatically add quotation marks.

dfn.description: Represents the defining instance of a term. The paragraph or description list containing `<dfn>` provides the definition.

abbr.description: Represents an abbreviation or acronym. The `title` attribute provides the full expansion.

code.description: Displays content as a fragment of computer code. Uses a monospace font by default. Wrap in `<pre>` for multi-line code blocks.

var.description: Represents a variable name in a mathematical expression or programming context.

samp.description: Represents sample (or quoted) output from a computer program.

kbd.description: Represents a span of inline text denoting textual user input from a keyboard or other input device.

sub.description: Specifies subscript text — displayed lower and smaller (e.g., chemical formulas: H₂O).

sup.description: Specifies superscript text — displayed higher and smaller (e.g., footnotes, exponents).

mark.description: Represents text highlighted for reference or notation purposes. Rendered with a yellow background by default.

time.description: Represents a specific time or date. The `datetime` attribute provides a machine-readable value.

br.description: Produces a line break in text. Should not be used for spacing — use CSS margin/padding instead.

wbr.description: Represents a word break opportunity — the browser may break the line at this point if needed.

forms.description: Elements for creating interactive forms to collect user input.

form.description: Represents a section containing interactive controls for submitting information. Key attributes: `action`, `method`, `enctype`, `novalidate`, `autocomplete`.

input.description: Creates interactive form controls. The `type` attribute determines behavior: text, password, email, number, checkbox, radio, file, date, range, color, hidden, submit, and more.

button.description: Represents a clickable button. Types: `submit` (form submission), `button` (custom JS), `reset` (form reset). Can contain HTML content unlike `<input type="button">`.

select.description: Represents a dropdown menu of options. Use `multiple` for multi-select. Group options with `<optgroup>`.

option.description: Defines an item in a `<select>`, `<optgroup>`, or `<datalist>`. The `value` is submitted; text content is displayed.

optgroup.description: Groups options within a `<select>` element. The `label` attribute provides a visible heading for the group.

textarea.description: Multi-line plain-text editing control. Control size with `rows`/`cols` or CSS. Supports `maxlength`, `minlength`, `placeholder`, `wrap`.

label.description: Associates a caption with a form control. Use the `for` attribute matching an `id`, or wrap the control inside the label. Improves accessibility and click targets.

fieldset.description: Groups related form controls together. Often used with `<legend>` for a caption. Can be `disabled` to disable all contained controls.

legend.description: Provides a caption for the content of its parent `<fieldset>`. Must be the first child of `<fieldset>`.

output.description: Represents the result of a calculation or user action. The `for` attribute references inputs that contributed to the result.

progress.description: Displays completion progress of a task. If no `value`, shows indeterminate state. Text content is fallback.

meter.description: Represents a scalar measurement within a known range (not progress — use `<progress>` for that). Supports `low`, `high`, `optimum`.

datalist.description: Contains `<option>` elements representing predefined suggestions for an `<input>`. Link via the input's `list` attribute.

media.description: Elements for embedding images, video, audio, and other media content.

img.description: Embeds an image. `src` and `alt` are required. Use `width`/`height` to prevent layout shift. Use `loading="lazy"` for below-fold images.

video.description: Embeds a media player for video playback. Key attributes: `controls`, `autoplay`, `muted`, `loop`, `poster`, `preload`. Use `<source>` for multiple formats.

audio.description: Embeds sound content. Supports `controls`, `autoplay`, `muted`, `loop`, `preload`. Use `<source>` for format fallbacks.

source.description: Specifies multiple media resources for `<picture>`, `<audio>`, or `<video>`. Browser selects the first supported source.

track.description: Specifies timed text tracks (subtitles, captions, descriptions) for `<video>` and `<audio>`. Uses WebVTT format.

picture.description: Contains `` elements and one `` fallback for responsive images. Browser selects the best source based on media queries, format, and resolution.

canvas.description: Container for drawing graphics via the Canvas 2D API or WebGL. Set dimensions via attributes. Content inside is fallback for unsupported browsers.

svg.description: Container for SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics). Resolution-independent. Supports shapes, paths, text, gradients, filters, animations, and interactivity.

iframe.description: Embeds another HTML page within the current page. Use `sandbox` to restrict, `allow` for permissions policy, `loading="lazy"` for performance.

embed.description: Embeds external content provided by an external application or plugin. Use `<iframe>` or other specific elements when possible.

object.description: Represents an external resource: image, nested browsing context, or plugin-handled resource. Largely replaced by specific elements.

map.description: Defines a client-side image map with clickable areas. Used with `` elements and linked via ``.

area.description: Defines clickable areas inside an image map. Shapes: `rect`, `circle`, `poly`, `default`.

interactive.description: Elements that provide interactive user interface components.

details.description: Creates a disclosure widget that can be toggled open/closed. Must contain a `<summary>` as the first child. The `open` attribute controls default state.

summary.description: Specifies the visible heading for a `<details>` widget. Clicking toggles the disclosure. Must be the first child of `<details>`.

dialog.description: Represents a dialog box or subwindow. Use `.showModal()` for modal dialogs with backdrop, `.show()` for modeless. Built-in focus trapping and Escape key handling.

table.description: Elements for creating and structuring tabular data.
Represents tabular data — information in a two-dimensional table. Use for data, not layout. Structure with `<thead>`, `<tbody>`, `<tfoot>`.

caption.description: Specifies the title of a table. Must be the first child of `<table>`. Helps screen readers understand table purpose.

thead.description: Groups the header content in a table. Contains rows with `<th>` header cells.

tbody.description: Groups the body content in a table. Multiple `<tbody>` elements can be used to group different sections.

tfoot.description: Groups the footer content in a table. Typically contains summary rows.

tr.description: Defines a row of cells in a table. Contains `<td>` and/or `<th>` elements.

th.description: Defines a header cell. Use `scope` attribute (col, row, colgroup, rowgroup) to associate with data cells. Supports `colspan` and `rowspan`.

td.description: Defines a standard data cell. Supports `colspan` and `rowspan` to span multiple columns or rows.

colgroup.description: Defines a group of columns within a table for applying styles or attributes together.

col.description: Defines column properties within a `<colgroup>`. The `span` attribute specifies how many columns the element encompasses.

div.description: The Content Division element. A generic container for flow content. It has no effect on content or layout until styled with CSS.

span.description: A generic inline container for phrasing content that does not inherently represent anything.

form.description: Represents a document section containing interactive controls for submitting information.

action.description: The URL that processes the form submission.

method.description: The HTTP method to submit the form with.

enctype.description: The MIME type of the form submission.

novalidate.description: Indicates the form should not be validated when submitted.

input.description: Used to create interactive controls for web-based forms to accept data from the user.

type.description: Type of form control to display.

name.description: Name of the form control. Submitted with the form as part of a name/value pair.

value.description: The initial value of the control.

placeholder.description: Text that appears in the form control when it has no value.

required.description: Indicates this field must be filled before form submission.

disabled.description: Whether the form control is disabled.

pattern.description: Pattern the form control's value should match.

button.description: An interactive element activated by a user with a mouse, keyboard, finger, voice command, or other assistive technology.

a.description: The Anchor element. Creates a hyperlink to web pages, files, email addresses, or anything else a URL can address.

href.description: The URL that the hyperlink points to.

target.description: Where to display the linked URL.

rel.description: The relationship of the linked URL.

download.description: Prompts the user to save the linked URL instead of navigating to it.

img.description: Embeds an image into the document.

loading.description: Indicates how the browser should load the image.

srcset.description: A set of image sources with width or pixel density descriptors.

script.description: Used to embed executable code or data; typically JavaScript.

style.description: Contains CSS style information for a document, or part of a document.

meta.description: Represents metadata that cannot be represented by other HTML meta-related elements.

link.description: Specifies relationships between the current document and an external resource. Most commonly used for stylesheets.

section.description: Represents a generic standalone section of a document which doesn't have a more specific semantic element to represent it.

article.description: Represents a self-contained composition which is intended to be independently distributable or reusable.

header.description: Represents introductory content, typically a group of introductory or navigational aids.

nav.description: Represents a section of a page whose purpose is to provide navigation links.

footer.description: Represents a footer for its nearest ancestor sectioning content or sectioning root element.

canvas.description: Used for drawing graphics via scripting (usually JavaScript). Can be used for animation, game graphics, data visualization, photo manipulation, and real-time video processing.

video.description: Embeds a media player which supports video playback into the document.

audio.description: Used to embed sound content in documents.

select.description: Represents a control that provides a menu of options.

textarea.description: Represents a multi-line plain-text editing control.

ul.description: Represents an unordered list of items, typically rendered as a bulleted list.

ol.description: Represents an ordered list of items, typically rendered as a numbered list.

li.description: Represents an item in a list.

details.description: Creates a disclosure widget in which information is visible only when toggled open.

dialog.description: Represents a dialog box or other interactive component, such as a confirmation or subwindow.

template.description: A mechanism for holding HTML that is not rendered when the page loads but may be instantiated via JavaScript.
